soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7576 — perf(dataset): reuse session-cached manifest when opening a dataset
zhangyue19921010 (core) · M (+45/-36, 5 files) · no escalation

SUBJECTIVE
  DatasetBuilder::load_by_uri always re-read the manifest from storage on the load-from-URI path, even when the same version was already resident on the session. Because lance-spark shares one Session per executor and every task opens the dataset independently, manifest reads scaled with task count, turning the manifest object into a read hotspot on object storage. This PR extracts a Dataset::get_manifest helper that consults the session metadata cache (keyed by version + e_tag) before loading, so the manifest is read once per executor and reused across tasks. CI green, mergeable.

OBJECTIVE (each tied to ground truth)
  - dataset.rs:753 (new get_manifest) — get-or-load-and-insert against session.metadata_cache.for_dataset(uri), keyed by ManifestKey { version, e_tag }. Exactly the logic previously inlined in load_new_transactions (base dataset.rs:2907-2930), hoisted verbatim. Behavior-preserving.
  - dataset.rs:2947 — load_new_transactions now calls get_manifest. dataset.metadata_cache = session.metadata_cache.for_dataset(&uri) in checkout_manifest, so passing dataset.session + &dataset.uri reconstructs the identical cache namespace. Same cache, same key.
  - builder.rs:869 — load-from-storage else branch routes through get_manifest; caller-supplied-manifest branch untouched. Return-type plumbing consistent: load_manifest returns owned Manifest, get_manifest wraps in Arc.
  - Dictionary-population safety (main concern) — cleared. load_manifest already runs populate_schema_dictionary when should_use_legacy_format() (dataset.rs:727-729), plus feature-flag validation and index/transaction co-caching. Cache stores a fully-realized manifest; a hit returns it already-populated; a miss still goes through load_manifest.
  - Cache-key soundness: ManifestKey.key() = manifest/{version}[/{e_tag}], namespaced per-URI via for_dataset's with_key_prefix(uri). Immutability-per-version is a pre-existing invariant; reuses the key load_new_transactions already used — no new keying risk.
  - Call-site scoping deliberate: checkout_by_ref (short-circuited by already_checked_out) and io/commit.rs:171 (Session::default) intentionally left uncached. Confirmed via grep + child sweep.
  - Tests: test_load_manifest_iops 2->1, commit test 5->4 — reflect manifest served from cache after a same-session write; residual IOP is the _versions list. index.rs adds session.file_metadata_cache().clear() — necessary so the newly-cached manifest doesn't defeat the forced-re-read assertion. No assertion loosened to hide a regression.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: low (down from baseline med) · urgency: low. A hoist of an already-proven caching block onto one more call path; the one real hazard (asymmetric dictionary population between builder branches) verified as a non-issue. escalation: none. could not read: none (0 omitted).

PLAN
  No blocking conditions. Optional follow-up (checkout_by_ref could reuse get_manifest) and a doc note on e_tag: None keying, both non-gating.

RECOMMENDATION: approve
  confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if some load_by_uri caller depends on the manifest being a fresh distinct allocation per open — but checkout_manifest takes Arc<Manifest> as immutable and load_new_transactions already shared cached Arcs.
summary: Hoists the existing session-metadata-cache get-or-load block (from load_new_transactions) into a Dataset::get_manifest helper and routes the load_by_uri storage path through it, so a manifest is read once per session/executor and reused across tasks. Verified behavior-preserving: load_manifest already populates schema dictionaries + validates feature flags internally, so cache hits return fully-realized manifests; the two remaining direct load_manifest callers (checkout_by_ref short-circuits, commit.rs uses a throwaway Session::default) are correctly left uncached; test IOP-count deltas match the new caching and none were loosened to mask a regression. Low risk, approve.

comments.label: praise
comments.body: praise: Good factoring. This is the exact get-or-load-and-insert block previously inlined in load_new_transactions, now hoisted into a single get_manifest helper and reused on the load_by_uri path — de-duplicates the cache logic and is behavior-preserving (same ManifestKey, same for_dataset(uri) namespace).

comments.label: question
comments.body: question (non-blocking): Confirming the dictionary-population invariant for the record — the caller-supplied-manifest branch above runs populate_schema_dictionary explicitly, while this load path relies on load_manifest doing it internally (dataset.rs:727-729). Since get_manifest caches the manifest returned after that population, cache hits are already-populated. Flagging only so a future reader doesn't re-derive it. No change requested.

comments.label: suggestion
comments.body: suggestion (non-blocking, out of scope): checkout_by_ref still calls load_manifest directly (bypassing the cache). The already_checked_out short-circuit covers the common case, but a checkout to a previously-loaded-but-not-current version would re-read. Could route through get_manifest in a follow-up. Leaving io/commit.rs:171 as-is is correct since it uses Session::default().
